outbound_official.notes: The Visa Advice Unit within the Consular Services Office of Malta's Ministry for Foreign and European Affairs operates an interactive per-country lookup tool at consularplus.gov.mt/visa-advice for Maltese ordinary passport holders. The site is a fully JavaScript-rendered SPA; no static downloadable country list is published. Country-level data cannot be scraped from this endpoint without a JS-capable browser; lists above are left empty pending a browser-based extraction. The tool replaced the older foreignandeu.gov.mt/en/Pages/Visa-Advice-for-Maltese-nationals.aspx page (now redirects to foreign.gov.mt). Contact: consular.mfea@gov.mt, tel +356 2204 2313 / 2204 2331.

visa_free.nationality: EU/EEA Member State nationals and Swiss nationals
visa_free.notes: All citizens of EU Member States (including other Schengen members) and EEA/Swiss nationals benefit from EU freedom of movement and have no visa requirement. Malta is a full Schengen member. The 29 Schengen Member States are: Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Czech Republic, Croatia, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovenia, Slovakia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland.
Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports. 90 days within any 180-day period.
Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports. 90 days within any 180-day period.
Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports. 90 days within any 180-day period.
Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports issued in line with ICAO standards. 90 days within any 180-day period.

Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports issued by Kosovo in line with ICAO standards. Effective 1 January 2024 under Regulation (EU) 2023/850. Kosovo is not universally recognised as a state. 90 days within any 180-day period.
Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports issued in line with ICAO standards. 90 days within any 180-day period.
Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports. 90 days within any 180-day period.

Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports. From 13 October 2024, this includes holders of Serbian passports issued by the Serbian Coordination Directorate (Koordinaciona uprava) — the previous exclusion was removed by Regulation (EU) 2024/2495. 90 days within any 180-day period.
Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports issued in line with ICAO standards. 90 days within any 180-day period.

UK nationals (British citizens who are nationals of the UK for purposes of Union law) are visa-exempt. Also includes: British nationals (Overseas), British overseas territories citizens (BOTC), British overseas citizens (BOC), British protected persons (BPP), British subjects (BS). 90 days within any 180-day period.
Special Administrative Region of China. Visa exemption applies only to holders of a 'Hong Kong Special Administrative Region' passport. 90 days within any 180-day period.
Special Administrative Region of China. Visa exemption applies only to holders of a 'Regiao Administrativa Especial de Macau' passport. 90 days within any 180-day period.

Territorial authority not recognised as a state by all EU member states. Visa exemption applies only to holders of passports issued by Taiwan which include an identity card number. 90 days within any 180-day period.

visa_policy.notes: Malta is a full Schengen Area member. Short-stay visa policy is governed by EU Regulation 2018/1806 (consolidated to 30 December 2025). All EU/EEA/Swiss nationals have unrestricted freedom of movement. Third-country nationals from Annex II countries are visa-exempt for up to 90 days in any 180-day period. All other third-country nationals require a Schengen visa (short stay, up to 90 days) or a National D-visa (long stay, 91-365 days). No e-visa or visa-on-arrival facility exists — visa applications must be submitted at a Visa Application Centre or Maltese diplomatic mission. ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) is expected to start operations in late 2026 and will apply to all visa-exempt third-country nationals. Key amendments since 2023: (1) Kosovo added to Annex II effective 1 January 2024 (Regulation (EU) 2023/850); (2) Serbia Coordination Directorate passport exclusion removed effective 13 October 2024 (Regulation (EU) 2024/2495); (3) Vanuatu removed from Annex II and added to Annex I (visa required) effective 3 February 2025 (Regulation (EU) 2025/11).

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen residence permit (Type C short-stay or Type D long-stay visa / national long-stay visa issued by another Schengen state)

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding a valid residence permit or long-stay visa (Type D national visa) issued by any Schengen Member State are authorised to enter Malta (and other Schengen states) for transit or short stays without an additional Schengen visa, provided the total stay does not exceed 90 days in any 180-day period and all other entry conditions of Article 6(1) of the Schengen Borders Code are met (valid travel document, sufficient means, no security threat). Entry is for transit to reach the issuing Schengen state or for short-stay visits. Other conditions of Art.6(1) of Regulation (EU) 2016/399 still apply.

Citizens of the People's Republic of China holding valid diplomatic or service passports (excluding Chinese 'public affairs' service passports per Article X of the agreement) may enter, exit or transit Malta visa-free for up to 30 days. Members of diplomatic/consular missions during their term of posting are also covered. Stays beyond 30 days require application through diplomatic channels. Officials at vice-ministerial level or above / major-general rank or above must notify Malta 5 working days before travel for official purposes. Agreement signed in Valletta on 18 December 2007; entry into force per notification procedure under Article X.

Qatari nationals holding valid diplomatic or special passports are exempt from visa requirements for Malta under the Agreement between the Government of Malta and the Government of the State of Qatar for the Exemption of Visa Requirements for Diplomatic and Special Passport Holders. Signed 14 April 2010; ratified 10 December 2011; in force 30 December 2011. Exact max stay days and further conditions stated in the treaty text (PDF).

Pakistani nationals holding valid diplomatic or official passports are exempt from visa requirements for Malta under the Agreement between Malta and Pakistan on Diplomatic Visa Waiver Agreements (Memorandum of Understanding). Signed 15 July 2016; ratified 1 October 2019; in force 19 December 2019. Full conditions in treaty text.

Algerian nationals holding valid diplomatic passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (information communicated by Member States to the Commission, 11 July 2023).

Bolivian nationals holding valid diplomatic or service/official passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Egyptian nationals holding valid diplomatic, service/official or special passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Indian nationals holding valid diplomatic passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023). Note: OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholders are NOT covered by this rule; no OCI-specific conditional access rule for Malta was found on official sources.

Indonesian nationals holding valid diplomatic or service/official passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Jordanian nationals holding valid diplomatic passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Kazakhstani nationals holding valid diplomatic passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Moroccan nationals holding valid diplomatic or service/official passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Omani nationals holding valid diplomatic, service/official or special passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Philippine nationals holding valid diplomatic or service/official passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Thai nationals holding valid diplomatic or service/official passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Tunisian nationals holding valid diplomatic or service/official passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Turkish nationals holding valid diplomatic or service/official passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

cbi.program_name: Granting of Citizenship by Naturalisation for Exceptional Services by Direct Investment (ESDI)

options.notes: Standard 36-month residence route: minimum direct investment of €600,000. Accelerated 12-month residence route: minimum direct investment of €750,000. Additionally, a minimum charitable donation of €10,000 is required to a registered philanthropic, cultural, sport, scientific, animal welfare or artistic NGO.
Residential property purchase of minimum €700,000, OR lease at minimum €16,000 per year for 5 years. Property must be held for at least 5 years.

cbi.processing_time: No fast-track option; no specific published timeline. All applications go through a rigorous four-tier due diligence process via licensed agents only.
cbi.notes: Regulated under S.L. 188.06 (Granting of Citizenship for Exceptional Services Regulations). Standard route requires 36 months of residence in Malta prior to application; accelerated route requires 12 months of residence combined with higher investment (€750,000). Ineligible nationalities: Afghanistan, Democratic Republic of Congo, Iran, North Korea, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Venezuela, Yemen. Malta permits dual citizenship since February 10, 2000. Note: Malta's previous golden passport/Individual Investor Programme (IIP) was the subject of a CJEU infringement ruling (April 2025); the ESDI programme as described here is the current active programme via Komunita Malta (komunita.gov.mt). The program is distinct from standard naturalization by residence (which requires minimum 5 years aggregate residence in the preceding 6 years and 12 consecutive months immediately before application).

programs.name: Malta Permanent Residence Programme (MPRP)
programs.notes: Available to non-EU, non-EEA, non-Swiss nationals. Grants permanent residency (not citizenship). Key financial requirements: (1) Administration fee: €40,000 (non-refundable €10,000 upfront + €30,000 after Letter of Approval in Principle). (2) Government contribution: €28,000 if purchasing property, or €58,000 if leasing property (covers main applicant, spouse, dependent children). Additional €7,500 per parent/grandparent dependant. (3) Property: Purchase qualifying property at minimum €300,000 (South Malta/Gozo) or €350,000 (rest of Malta), OR lease at minimum €10,000/year (South Malta/Gozo) or €12,000/year (rest of Malta). (4) NGO donation: €2,000 to a local registered NGO. (5) Proof of capital: Option A — €500,000 total assets (of which minimum €150,000 must be financial assets such as stocks, bonds, bank deposits); Option B — €650,000 total assets (of which minimum €75,000 must be in financial assets). Assets monitored annually for first 5 years. Ineligible: Afghanistan, North Korea, Iran, DRC, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Yemen, Venezuela, Russia, Belarus. Processing: decisions within a 'reasonable timeframe'. Regulated by S.L. 217.26. Residence certificate does not expire as long as obligations are maintained.

programs.name: Malta Residence and Visa Programme (MRVP)
programs.notes: Separate from the MPRP; grants Schengen travel rights with Maltese residency. Full investment amounts not extractable from public-facing official pages — see S.L. 217.18 and MRVP FAQs (PDF). Eligible for four generations of family members. Investment must be held for minimum 5 years.

programs.name: Global Residence Programme (GRP)
programs.notes: Tax-residency programme for non-EU, non-EEA, non-Swiss nationals not already long-term residents. Provides flat 15% tax rate on foreign-sourced income remitted to Malta (35% on locally-sourced income). Administrative fee: €5,500 if qualifying property is in South Malta; €6,000 otherwise. Applicants must purchase or lease a qualifying property in Malta. Regulated by Malta Tax & Customs Administration (MTCA, formerly CFR).

programs.name: Nomad Residence Permit
programs.notes: Available to non-EU/EEA/Swiss third-country nationals who work remotely. Minimum gross annual income: €42,000 (for applications from 1 April 2024 onwards; previous threshold was €32,400). Applicants must work for a foreign-registered employer under contract, or be a partner/shareholder of a foreign-registered company, or provide freelance/consulting services to foreign-based clients. Initial permit: 1 year; renewable up to 3 times (maximum 4 years total). Requires valid travel document, health insurance covering EU risks, property rental/purchase agreement, police certificate.

programs.name: Key Employee Initiative (KEI)
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals with offer for a managerial or highly technical position, OR startup roles endorsed by Malta Enterprise. Minimum annual gross salary: €45,000. Must provide certified qualifications, warrants or proof of work experience. Employer must advertise the role for at least 2 weeks within 2 months prior to application.
programs.processing_time: 5 working days from complete application submission
programs.notes: Initial permit valid 1 year; renewable up to 3 years. Fee: €600 first application, €150/year renewal. Managed by Identità (formerly Identity Malta).

programs.name: Specialist Employee Initiative (SEI)
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals not meeting KEI criteria. Minimum annual gross salary: €30,000. Requires MQF Level 6+ qualification OR lower-level qualification plus 3+ years relevant experience (proven via contracts/references).
programs.processing_time: Not specified (standard Single Permit process applies)

programs.name: EU Blue Card
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals with advanced professional qualifications and high-salary job offers in EU countries including Malta.
programs.notes: Issued under EU Blue Card Directive. Managed by Identità.

programs.name: Malta Startup Residence Programme
programs.eligibility: Non-EU, non-EEA, non-Swiss startup founders, co-founders, and core employees. Startup must be registered no longer than 7 years globally. Must engage in eligible sectors (manufacturing, software, health/biotech, eco, other innovative activities). Minimum paid-up share capital of €25,000 (plus €10,000 per additional co-founder beyond 4, max 6 co-founders). Core employees must earn at least €30,000 gross annually. Business plan must be approved by Malta Enterprise.
programs.notes: Founders/co-founders receive a 3-year permit renewable for 5 further years. Core employees receive a 3-year permit renewable for 3 further years. Application fee: €750/adult applicant for 3-year permit + €300 residence card issuance fee + €690 Jobsplus employment licence. Ineligible: nationals/close ties to Afghanistan, North Korea, Iran, DRC, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Yemen, Venezuela. Jointly administered by Residency Malta Agency and Malta Enterprise.

validation_notes: ADDED Kosovo (XKX) to visa_free: Regulation (EU) 2023/850 transferred Kosovo from Annex I to Annex II effective 1 January 2024. The prior record noted this as a gap; it is now corrected and sourced to the official EU regulation.

REMOVED Vanuatu (VUT) from visa_free: Regulation (EU) 2025/11 (effective ~3 February 2025) moved Vanuatu from Annex II (visa-free) to Annex I (visa required) due to investor citizenship scheme security concerns. Vanuatu entry deleted.

CORRECTED Serbia (SRB) notes: Regulation (EU) 2024/2495 (effective 13 October 2024) removed the exclusion of Serbian Coordination Directorate (Koordinaciona uprava) passport holders. All holders of Serbian biometric passports are now visa-free. Source updated to official EUR-Lex regulation.

UPDATED MPRP capital requirement in rbi notes: Added Option B (€650,000 total assets with minimum €75,000 in financial assets) alongside the existing Option A (€500,000 with €150,000 financial), confirmed from residencymalta.gov.mt January 2025 brochure search results.
UPDATED visa_policy.notes: Added ETIAS expected start (late 2026), summarized three key post-2023 Annex II amendments (Kosovo, Serbia, Vanuatu) with regulation references.
UPDATED visa_policy.source_urls: Added consolidated Regulation 2018/1806 (20251230), Regulation 2023/850 (Kosovo), Regulation 2024/2495 (Serbia), Regulation 2025/11 (Vanuatu).
UPDATED data_quality.gaps: Resolved the Kosovo gap (now recorded); updated Vanuatu status.
gaps: Visa-on-arrival: Malta has no visa-on-arrival facility; none to record.
E-visa: Malta has no e-visa system; none to record.

ETA: Malta has no ETA system currently; ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) expected to launch late 2026 and will apply to visa-exempt ordinary passport holders — not yet operational.
Full investment amounts for the Malta Residence and Visa Programme (MRVP / S.L. 217.18) not extractable from public official pages; amounts omitted from min_amount field.
MPRP processing time not specified with a number of days/months on official pages — only described as 'reasonable timeframe'.
Citizenship by Exceptional Services (ESDI) processing time not specified numerically on official pages.

Conditional access - passport_type: The EU OJ 2023 notice (CELEX:52023XC0927(01)) is the most recent official list of Malta national derogations checked; the 2022 notice additionally listed Angola, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cambodia, Iran, Mongolia, and Qatar diplomatic/service passports — these were not present in 2023 notice, suggesting removal or non-confirmation; not recorded here due to uncertainty about current status. Ghana bilateral visa waiver agreement referenced in press sources but no confirmed treaty page on foreign.gov.mt was accessible; not recorded.

Conditional access - credential: No official Malta or EU source was found granting visa-free or reduced-requirement entry to Malta based solely on holding a valid US, UK, Australian, Canadian, or other non-Schengen visa or residence permit. No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder-specific rule for entry to Malta found on any official source.
Conditional access - special_status: No Malta-specific OCI or other diaspora/heritage special status entry rules were found on official sources.
Max stay days for Qatar (QAT) and Pakistan (PAK) diplomatic passport treaty entries: full treaty PDF texts not successfully extracted; max_stay_days set to null pending PDF access.

A more recent version of the Identità visa-free country list PDF may have been issued after October 2023; the official PDF URL used as primary source has not changed but may not reflect post-2023 amendments (Kosovo, Serbia Coord. Dir., Vanuatu) — those are sourced directly from EU regulations.
